description: chore(deps) keeps dep bumps out of the release CHANGELOG (release-please hides chore); see CLAUDE.md release ritual. No schedule — PRs open as soon as a release clears minimumReleaseAge; all bumps (including majors) automerge once CI is green. The repo is public, so GitHub-native auto-merge (platformAutomerge) gates on the `ci-success` required status check set by main's branch protection (enforce_admins=false, so admin direct-to-main pushes bypass it; the Renovate bot does not). Strong CI/test coverage makes the green pipeline a sufficient guard for major bumps too.

packageRules.description: Batch all non-major Go module bumps into one PR — most are transitive churn not worth a PR each.
OpenTelemetry packages release in lockstep — group across all update types so a bump is coherent (and never a partial set).
Kubernetes client/apimachinery libs are co-versioned — bumping one without the others breaks the build.

gRPC + protobuf are tightly coupled. Deliberately NOT restricted to tagged-only protobuf releases: an untagged google.golang.org/protobuf pseudo-version dragged in transitively by a k8s.io/* bump is accepted as long as the full CI suite — including the `acceptance` job, which re-validates the gateway/emit encode path (CP-M1) — passes green on that bump's PR before automerge. See followup.md's build-decision ledger (the retired "k8s.io/* pinned to v0.35.6" row, 2026-07-03) for the full history and the re-pin fallback if a regression is ever traced to a pseudo-version.
The golang builder image must track go.mod's Go version — bump it with the toolchain, separately from the distroless runtime base.
CI actions (Forgejo/GitHub) grouped — they're all major-pinned tags.
